title: juniperus cedrus subsp. maderensis
intro: Juniperus cedrus subsp. maderensis, also known as Madeiran cedar, is a subspecies of the Atlas cedar, a coniferous tree native to the island of Madeira in the Atlantic Ocean. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Madeiran cedar include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving circulation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The herbal preparations of Juniperus cedrus subsp. maderensis include essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
